Intro_Holding_0: September 18, 9:24 AM{nl}District Court - Defendant Lobby No. 2
Intro_Holding_1: Nick, how are things looking?{nl}Aren't you nervous?
Intro_Holding_3: We are fully prepared.{nl}All that's left is to reveal the truth.
Intro_Holding_5: It's going to be fine.{nl}Leave it to me.
Intro_Holding_7: (Alright, the trial is about to start.{nl}I'd better check the {color:red}Court Record{color:lightblue} one more time.)
Court_Opening_2: Court is now in session for the trial of{nl}Maggey Byrde.
Court_Opening_3: The defense is ready,{p:150} Your Honor.
Court_Opening_5: The defendant has practically confessed.{nl}Let us keep this brief.
Court_Opening_6: Very well, {p:150}Mr. Edgeworth.{nl}Your opening statement, please.
Court_Opening_7: ...{p:300}The incident took place at Joypolis.{nl}It occurred on September 17.
Court_Opening_8: The victim, Kurumi Nakaki, who worked there as a suit actor,{nl}was found dead.
Court_Opening_9: The location was the 1st-floor stage of the event hall.
Court_Opening_10: Right before the eyes of the first discoverer, Frank Sahwit,{nl}the defendant pushed the victim from the 4th floor.
Court_Opening_11: The victim and the defendant were on bad terms.{nl}Everyone at the theater knew this.
Court_Opening_12: Furthermore, the defendant has admitted{nl}to pushing her.
Court_Opening_13: The defendant never explicitly said that!
Court_Opening_14: She merely thinks that, given the flow of events,{nl}she might have pushed her by accident!
Court_Opening_15: What difference does it make?{nl}It amounts to the exact same thing.
Court_Opening_18: The time the body was discovered by the first witness{nl}was 10:05 PM.
Court_Opening_19: The estimated time of death is between{nl}9:30 PM and 10:30 PM.
Court_Opening_20: The cause of death was a severe blow to the back of the head{nl}due to falling from the 4th floor. It was a cerebral contusion.
Court_Opening_21: The details are recorded in the autopsy report.{nl}That is all.
Court_Opening_22: Please add the data from the {Autopsy Report}{nl}to the Court Record.
Court_Opening_23: Today, I have summoned the first discoverer{nl}who witnessed all of this.
Court_Opening_24: Understood.{nl}Mr. Edgeworth, please call your witness to the stand.
Court_Opening_25: The event hall supervisor at Joypolis,{nl}Frank Sahwit.
Court_Opening_26: ~The Situation at the Time of the Incident~
Court_Opening_27: On the day of the incident, I told the two of them to be on the{nl}4th floor of the event hall at 10:00 PM.
Court_Opening_28: After finishing my work, I sat down on the 1st-floor stage{nl}and waited for the time to come.
Court_Opening_29: When I happened to look up at the 4th floor,{nl}I saw the victim and the defendant.
Court_Opening_30: Then, the defendant approached the victim without any hesitation{nl}and pushed her right off!
Court_Opening_31: Your Honor, what do you think?{nl}Is the defendant's guilt not obvious?
Court_Opening_32: Hold on a second!{nl}The trial has only just begun!
Court_Opening_33: ...{p:300}Wait, could it be that Mr. Edgeworth took a liking to Joypolis{nl}during his investigation?
Court_Opening_34: And you're thinking about wrapping up the trial quickly{nl}so you can go play?
Court_Opening_35: Oho, {p:400}an amusement park that even catches Mr. Edgeworth's interest?{nl}I would like to go there myself.
Court_Opening_36: What nonsense are you spouting?{nl}It is nothing of the sort.
Court_Opening_37: It simply means that this trial is so clear-cut{nl}that it will be over shortly.
Court_Opening_38: (Like hell I'll let it end quickly.{nl}There's no way Maggey would commit murder!)
First_Testimony_Start_0: ~The Situation at the Time of the Incident~
Press_Y1_0_1: Why did you call the two of them there?
Press_Y1_0_2: Because the defendant and the victim were on bad terms{nl}and frequently had heated arguments.
Press_Y1_0_3: The two of them were...{p:200} well... so hostile towards each other{nl}that they could have started killing each other at any moment.
Press_Y1_0_4: However...{p:200} since they were both very dedicated to their work,{nl}it was a major headache for me as their supervisor.
Press_Y1_0_5: That is why I decided to step in{nl}and have them talk things out.
Press_Y1_1_1: The meeting was on the 4th floor, right?{nl}Why did you wait on the 1st floor?
Press_Y1_1_2: The 1st-floor stage was the best place{nl}to sit and wait.
Press_Y1_1_3: What time did you arrive at the 1st floor?
Press_Y1_1_4: I believe I sat down on the stage{nl}around 9:15 PM.
Press_Y1_1_5: I intended to stay on the 1st floor{nl}and go up to the 4th floor when the time came.
Press_Y1_1_6: But in the end, I never actually{nl}went up to the 4th floor that day.
Press_Y1_1_7: You sat on the 1st floor at around 9:15 PM,{nl}and the meeting was at 10:00 PM.
Press_Y1_1_8: What were you doing on the 1st floor{nl}for those 45 minutes?
Press_Y1_1_9: I was desperately trying to figure out{nl}how to improve their relationship.
Press_Y1_1_10: But I couldn't come up with any good ideas...{p:200}{nl}and before I knew it, it was 10:00 PM.
Press_Y1_2_1: It must have been quite dark inside the building at the time.{nl}You could clearly see the two of them on the 4th floor?
Press_Y1_2_2: My eyesight is actually quite good.{nl}I have 20/20 vision in both eyes without glasses.
Press_Y1_2_3: However, I didn't identify them{nl}just by sight alone.
Press_Y1_2_4: Right when I thought about going up to the 4th floor,{nl}I heard women arguing.
Press_Y1_2_5: When I looked up, there were two silhouettes{nl}arguing near the 4th-floor railing...
Press_Y1_2_6: I immediately knew from their voices.{nl}That it was the defendant and the victim.
Press_Y1_2_7: ...{p:300}But to my shame, I could only{nl}watch helplessly from the 1st floor.
Press_Y1_3_2: How did you know the defendant was the one who pushed her{nl}in that darkness?
Press_Y1_3_4: From their voices, I knew it was the victim and the defendant{nl}arguing on the 4th floor.
Press_Y1_3_5: And the one who fell was the victim.{nl}Therefore, the one who pushed her must have been the defendant.
CE1_Hint_0: Hold on, Nick!{nl}This is the important part! Calm down!
CE1_Hint_1: (She's right. Calm down...{p:200} I need to stay calm.{nl}This testimony clearly has a contradiction!)
CE1_Hint_2: (But how should I break it apart...?)
CE1_Hint_3: Defense,{p:150} the defendant's guilt is obvious.{nl}You should stop struggling and admit defeat.
CE1_Hint_4: Then the head and co-head of the law offices{nl}can go have a fun time at the amusement park.
CE1_Hint_5: Prosecutor Edgeworth...{p:200} Is he still holding a grudge{nl}over what I said earlier...?
CE1_Hint_7: (...Maggey said she reached her hand out{nl}without knowing what was in the darkness.)
CE1_Hint_8: (That must be because Maggey{nl}didn't have THAT with her at the time.)
CE1_Victory_2: Your Honor.{nl}Please take a look at this.
CE1_Victory_4: This is a photo of the defendant's glasses,{nl}taken before the incident.
CE1_Victory_5: Actually, these glasses were lost right before the incident.{nl}It was around 9:30 PM.
CE1_Victory_7: I-It's true, sir! While I was taking a shower in the waiting room,{nl}they completely disappeared, sir!
CE1_Victory_8: They are still missing even now.{nl}The ones I'm wearing are my spares, sir!
CE1_Victory_9: Her eyesight is extremely poor.{nl}Furthermore, she has astigmatism.
CE1_Victory_10: According to the testimony, the defendant approached the victim{nl}without hesitation and pushed her.
CE1_Victory_11: But would that even be possible{nl}for someone with such bad eyesight?
CE1_Victory_12: There's a chance she could have fallen over herself.{nl}Isn't that unnatural?
CE1_Victory_14: The defense does have a point.
CE1_Victory_17: The loss of the glasses is merely the defendant's claim.{nl}Do you have any concrete proof?
CE1_Victory_20: Maybe he's imagining himself playing at the amusement park...
CE1_Victory_21: The defendant walked right up to the railing.{nl}That is clearly established by the witness testimony.
CE1_Victory_22: In other words, she was actually wearing her glasses.
CE1_Victory_23: And she walked normally through the darkness{nl}and shoved the victim over the railing.
CE1_Victory_25: My glasses really did disappear, sir.{nl}I really was walking blind, sir!
CE1_Victory_26: Then perhaps you had your spare glasses with you at the time?
CE1_Victory_27: I only keep my spares at home, sir!
CE1_Victory_28: But you were able to walk to the railing.
CE1_Victory_29: ...{p:300}Y-Yes.{p:200}{nl}I managed to walk somehow without my glasses, sir...
CE1_Victory_30: Please continue.{nl}Since we are on the topic, let us hear the rest.
CE1_Victory_31: ...{p:300}I was walking blindly without my glasses, sir.{nl}Then I saw what looked like a faint shadow...
CE1_Victory_32: I called out, but there was no answer,{nl}so I reached my hand out, sir.
CE1_Victory_33: Then I heard the sound of something falling below{nl}and Mr. Sahwit's scream, sir...
CE1_Victory_36: Nick...{p:200}{nl}We're going to lose at this rate...
CE1_Victory_37: Let us proceed to the verdict,{nl}and we can all head out to the amusement park...
CE1_Victory_39: T-The {p:200}defendant... might have dropped something else{nl}instead of the victim!
CE1_Victory_42: She dropped something else, yet a dead body{nl}fell in front of the witness. That makes no sense.
CE1_Victory_43: This is not an amusement park attraction, you know.
CE1_Victory_47: The defense is trying to make this murder look like an accident{nl}by pretending the defendant couldn't see.
CE1_Victory_48: You have no proof of that either!
CE1_Victory_51: The witness has submitted photos taken at the time.{nl}Everything is captured right there.
CE1_Victory_52: Photos?{nl}I haven't heard anything about this...
CE1_Victory_54: Uuuugh.{p:200}{nl}Let's just hear what he has to say for now.
Second_Testimony_Start_1: This is the sequence of photos{nl}I took at that moment.
Second_Testimony_Start_2: I heard voices arguing,{nl}so I instinctively took out my cell phone and took some pictures.
Second_Testimony_Start_3: Here, please look.{nl}The defendant is pushing the victim as she sits on the railing.
Second_Testimony_Start_4: From the looks of it,{nl}the victim must have landed face-first on the ground.
Second_Testimony_Start_5: Well?{nl}Do you not think this is an obvious case of murder?
Second_Testimony_Start_7: Nick, you can't lose your nerve.{nl}We have to keep pushing forward for Maggey's sake!
Second_Testimony_Start_8: No matter how tough the situation is,{nl}there has to be a clue somewhere!
Press_Y2_0_1: Did you really ready your camera instinctively?{nl}It seems a bit too well-prepared to me.
Press_Y2_0_3: Taking out a cell phone and putting it in camera mode{nl}isn't exactly a difficult task, you know.
Press_Y2_1_1: What kind of argument did you hear?
Press_Y2_1_3: I heard the defendant's voice saying,{nl}'I told you to stop practicing after closing hours, sir.'
Press_Y2_1_4: Then the victim said, 'I'm not causing you any trouble,{nl}so stop nagging me.'
Press_Y2_1_5: Then the defendant yelled, 'If you won't listen,{nl}I'll just have to use force, sir!'...
Press_Y2_1_7: That's a lie, sir!{nl}I never said a single word of that, sir!
Press_Y2_1_8: I mean, I didn't say anything at all;{nl}I just walked up to the railing, sir!
Press_Y2_1_9: Then I vaguely thought I saw someone there,{nl}so I just quietly called out to them, sir!
Press_Y2_1_10: How pathetic.{nl}I clearly heard it with my own two ears.
Press_Y2_2_1: Only the legs are visible in the photo. How can you be sure{nl}that it's the defendant?
Press_Y2_2_2: It's a fact that the defendant walked up to the railing.{nl}There is no doubt that these are the defendant's legs.
Press_Y2_2_4: (I don't have a comeback for that,{nl}but I'll try to put on a brave face.)
Press_Y2_2_5: Nick.{nl}Just making a fierce face isn't going to cut it.
Press_Y2_2_6: This is a courtroom,{nl}so you need to respond with your words.
Press_Y2_3_2: The cause of death was a cerebral contusion{nl}from a severe blow to the back of the head!
Press_Y2_3_3: Ah, my apologies.{nl}This is a bit hard to understand without an explanation.
Press_Y2_3_4: The falling victim smashed{nl}face-first into the floor.
Press_Y2_3_5: The next moment, the impact caused her to flip over,{nl}and then she struck the back of her head severely.
Press_Y2_3_6: A-And that became the fatal wound?
Press_Y2_3_7: I hear the back of the head is more vulnerable to impact than the forehead.{nl}A cerebral contusion caused by hitting the back of the head is entirely possible.
Press_Y2_3_8: It probably wasn't as strong as the initial impact,{nl}but it looked like quite a heavy blow.
Press_Y2_3_9: ...{p:300}Hmm. That is crucial information.{nl}Please add it to your testimony.
Press_Y2_4_3: (The injury on the back of the victim's head{nl}couldn't have happened like that...)
Press_Y2_4_4: (If the testimony is true, then there's a massive{nl}contradiction with THAT piece of evidence!)
Press_Y2_4_5: Your Honor. The defense is ready to present a piece of evidence{nl}that exposes the contradiction in the testimony.
Press_Y2_4_8: (If I nail this,{nl}the tide should completely turn in our favor...)
CE2_Hint_0: Nick. If she hit the back of her head,{nl}wouldn't there be some kind of mark on the back of the mask the victim was wearing?
CE2_Present_mask_0: (The evidence pointing out the contradiction in his testimony{nl}is THIS!)
CE2_WrongEvidence_2: N-Nothing...{nl}I was just thinking about Joypolis...
CE2_WrongEvidence_3: Nick, we can all go there together{nl}after the trial is over!
CE2_WrongEvidence_4: Oh, right. I lost my focus for a second...
CE2_WrongEvidence_5: Defense. We are in the middle of a trial.{nl}Please answer properly within the time limit.
CE2_Victory_2: This is the Steel Samurai mask{nl}the victim was wearing at the time of the incident.
CE2_Victory_4: If she flipped over and hit the back of her head like you testified,{nl}wouldn't the topknot be broken?
CE2_Victory_6: But as you can see, the topknot is completely intact.
CE2_Victory_7: On top of that, there isn't a single scratch or dent{nl}on the back of the mask!
CE2_Victory_8: The witness's current testimony is full of contradictions!
CE2_Victory_11: ...{p:300}I see there is a contradiction{nl}in the testimony.
CE2_Victory_12: Then how does the defense believe{nl}the victim struck the back of her head?
CE2_Victory_13: I will explain.{nl}This is what happened.
CE2_Option_Menu_0: (How the victim struck{nl}the back of her head...)
CE2_Option_Menu_1: (That is proven by the fact that{nl}the back of the mask is undamaged...)
CE2_Option_Timer_1: N-Nothing...{nl}I was just thinking about Joypolis...

CE2_Option_Wrong_2_2: Nick... I don't think this is the time{nl}to be making terrible puns.
CE2_Option_Wrong_3_0: I said, the victim wore the mask massive...{nl}Wore the massive mask...
CE2_Option_Wrong_3_1: Nick... I don't think this is the time{nl}to be making terrible puns.
CE2_Option_MiaHint_0: (This is bad...{p:200} What would Mia think{nl}in a situation like this?)
CE2_Option_MiaHint_1: The victim had an injury on the back of her head,{nl}but the back of the mask had no damage.
CE2_Option_MiaHint_2: In other words, the victim was in a state where{nl}she wasn't wearing the mask...
CE2_Option_Correct_0: The victim was dropped while she wasn't wearing the mask,{nl}and that's when she received the severe blow to the back of her head!
CE2_Option_Correct_1: ...{p:300}Hmph.{p:200}{nl}I am so appalled I have no words.
CE2_Option_Correct_2: The photo of her falling proves{nl}that the victim WAS wearing the mask.
CE2_Option_Correct_3: (But then why would she flip over{nl}and leave the back of the mask undamaged...?)
CE2_Option_Correct_4: (Damn it...{p:200}{nl}I was so close to the truth of the incident...)
CE2_Option_Correct_7: Nick, when you're in trouble, calm down{nl}and organize your information. That's the key!
CE2_Option_Correct_8: (Organize my information?{nl}...{p:200}Maybe I should review the evidence again.)
CE2_Option_Correct_11: (Why was there such a loud noise...?{nl}That is a huge clue!)
CE2_Option_Correct_12: (Maya, thank you.{nl}I feel like buying you a year's supply of miso ramen.)
CE2_Option_Correct_14: It's true that the photo of her falling proves{nl}the victim was wearing the mask.
CE2_Option_Correct_16: That's not the time{nl}I'm talking about!
CE2_Option2_Wrong_1_0: The victim willingly fell on her own!
CE2_Option_Wrong_1_2: Ummm...{p:200} Because she really liked{nl}high places... maybe?
CE2_Option2_Wrong_1_3: Nick... that's taking things{nl}a bit too far!
CE2_Option_Wrong_3_2: Yes! The first time on her face, the second time on the back of her head,{nl}and the third time...{p:200} um...
CE2_Option2_Wrong_3_3: Nick... falling a third time{nl}is really stretching it, don't you think?
CE2_Option2_Timer_0: Defense?{nl}What's the matter, zoning out like that?
CE2_Option2_Timer_1: N-Nothing...{nl}I was just organizing my thoughts...
CE2_Option2_Timer_2: We are in the middle of a trial.{nl}Please answer properly within the time limit.
CE2_Option2_MiaHint_0: (This is bad...{p:200} What would Mia think{nl}in a situation like this?)
CE2_Option2_MiaHint_1: Nick. Think back to the 'loud noise'{nl}that Maggey heard around 9:30 PM.
CE2_Option2_MiaHint_2: What if something else fell at that time,{nl}other than the victim falling at 10:05 PM?
CE2_Option2_MiaHint_3: Who's to say the victim only fell once?
CE2_Option2_Correct_0: Isn't it possible the victim was dropped without the mask beforehand,{nl}and suffered the fatal blow to the back of the head then?
CE2_Option2_Correct_2: Look at the autopsy report.{nl}The estimated time of death is between 9:30 PM and 10:30 PM.
CE2_Option2_Correct_3: That means there is nothing strange about the victim{nl}being killed before 10:05 PM.
CE2_Option2_Correct_4: The defendant stated that she heard a loud noise{nl}outside the waiting room at around 9:30 PM.
CE2_Option2_Correct_5: The victim must have been dropped and killed{nl}at that precise moment!
CE2_Option2_Correct_7: And then at 10:05 PM, she fell face-first a second time,{nl}this time wearing the mask.
CE2_Option2_Correct_8: This is proven by the fact that only the eyebrow section{nl}of the mask is dented.
CE2_Option2_Correct_9: ...Thinking about it this way, the cause of death{nl}and the undamaged back of the mask perfectly align!
CE2_Option2_Correct_11: Then it becomes a question of{nl}who dropped her beforehand...
CE2_Option2_Correct_12: The defendant was taking a shower{nl}in the waiting room starting around 9:30 PM.
CE2_Option2_Correct_13: Meaning the defendant didn't commit any murder!{nl}The real culprit is someone else!
CE2_Option2_Correct_14: Is this not the true reality of the incident!?
CE2_Option2_Correct_16: (...{p:300}Alright! I feel like I just took{nl}a massive step closer to the truth!)
CE2_Option2_Correct_17: Way to go, Nick!{nl}Talk about a turnabout!
CE2_Option2_Correct_18: That is nothing but conjecture! You cannot prove{nl}that she fell before putting on the mask!
CE2_Option2_Correct_19: ...{p:300}Hmm. Let me ask one thing as well.{nl}Why are you concluding that she was 'dropped'?
CE2_Option2_Correct_20: Even if she had been murdered beforehand,{nl}could there not be other methods of murder?
CE2_Option2_Correct_21: You make a fair point, Your Honor.
CE2_Option2_Correct_22: But I believe going out of their way to drop her twice{nl}reveals the real culprit's intentions and circumstances.
CE2_Option2_Correct_23: Your Honor. I request to cross-examine{nl}Mr. Sahwit one more time.
CE2_Option2_Correct_24: I intend to prove everything,{nl}including that very point.
CE2_Option2_Correct_25: A-Are we still continuing?{nl}Isn't it about time for the verdict...?
CE2_Option2_Correct_26: Mr. Sahwit.{nl}What are you so afraid of?
CE2_Option2_Correct_27: Your hair has been twitching unnaturally for a while now...{p:200}{nl}You're acting very strangely!
CE2_Option2_Correct_28: It is true that his hair{nl}frequently shows unnatural movements.
CE2_Option2_Correct_29: But that has absolutely zero causal relationship{nl}with him acting strangely!
CE2_Option2_Correct_30: The defense attorney is suspecting me as the culprit!{nl}I find that highly offensive!
CE2_Option2_Correct_32: Please allow me to cross-examine Mr. Sahwit regarding his{nl}blank 50 minutes between 9:15 PM and the incident.
CE2_Option2_Correct_33: If we do that, we should find out whether Mr. Sahwit is truly innocent,{nl}and whether my theory is correct.
CE2_Option2_Correct_35: I will allow the continued cross-examination of Mr. Sahwit.
CE2_Option2_Correct_37: If you're going to doubt me that much,{nl}I will prove my innocence out of sheer pride!
Third_Testimony_Start_1: I finished my work around 9:15 PM,{nl}and after that, I stayed on the 1st-floor stage the entire time.
Third_Testimony_Start_2: I finished my work around 9:15 PM,{nl}and after that, I stayed on the 1st-floor stage the entire time.
Third_Testimony_Start_3: You might suspect me, but{nl}you are making a massive misunderstanding.
Third_Testimony_Start_4: I never went to the 4th floor.
Third_Testimony_Start_5: There is no way{nl}I could have pushed anyone.
Third_Testimony_Start_6: Nick, what should we do?{nl}His testimony is so vague...
Press_Y3_0_5: Is it really, really, really true?
Press_Y3_0_6: That's enough, you two.{nl}This is going nowhere.
Press_Y3_1_1: To clear up these suspicions,{nl}could you speak in more detail?
Press_Y3_1_2: About the blank 50 minutes between 9:15 PM{nl}and the incident, {p:250}{courtSprite:defense["Pointing"]}in full detail!
Press_Y3_1_4: I was entirely focused on thinking about{nl}how to mend the relationship between the victim and the defendant.
Press_Y3_1_6: Are you sure?{nl}You didn't do something like worry about your hair?
Press_Y3_1_7: W-What are you talking about?{nl}That makes no sense!
Press_Y3_2_1: Do you have any proof that you didn't go there?{nl}You don't, do you?
Press_Y3_2_2: I-I certainly do!{nl}The sequence of photos I showed you earlier!
Press_Y3_2_3: If you look at those, it's obvious{nl}that I was on the 1st floor!
Press_Y3_2_4: That only serves as proof{nl}for around 10:05 PM!
Press_Y3_2_5: What about the time before that?
Press_Y3_2_6: Just as I said!{nl}I was on the 1st floor the entire time before that too!
Press_Y3_2_7: ...Hmm. That is crucial information.{nl}Please add it to your testimony.
Press_Y3_3_1: A noise loud enough for the showering defendant to hear,{nl}and it only happened once...?
Press_Y3_4_1: How can you be so certain?
Press_Y3_4_2: B-Because it is firmly{nl}etched into my head!
Press_Y3_4_3: Into that head with the hair{shake:300}{playSound:hit2}{courtSprite:defense["Pointing"]}that twitches so unnaturally!?
Press_Y3_4_4: W-What are you saying!{nl}It is etched into my memory!
Press_Y3_4_5: I will repeat myself!{nl}Between 9:15 PM and the incident, there was not a single strange...
Press_Y3_4_6: Between 9:15 PM and the incident,{nl}there was not a single strange occurrence!
Press_Y3_4_9: Your Honor, the defense wishes to present evidence{nl}pointing out the contradiction in that testimony.
CE3_Hint_0: Could you repeat your testimony?{nl}In more detail, if possible.
CE3_Hint_1: In more detail?{nl}I am doing my utmost best.
CE3_Hint_2: Lining up vague statements{nl}won't prove your innocence, you know!
CE3_Hint_3: Hmph.{p:200}{nl}No need to raise your voice.
CE3_Hint_4: Look,{p:150} Nick.{nl}Prosecutor Edgeworth is smiling again...
CE3_Hint_5: The situation is getting tough, so maybe he's escaping reality{nl}by fantasizing about playing at the amusement park.
CE3_Hint_6: (...{p:300}No, that's not it.{nl}That is a smile of confidence.)
CE3_Hint_7: (Does he still have a trump card...?)
CE3_Hint_8: (I need to stay calm{nl}and hear what he has to say...)
CE3_Present_Evidence_0: (Mr. Sahwit claims nothing strange happened.{nl}But actually, something did!)
CE3_Present_Evidence_1: (If I can get him to explain this evidence,{nl}everything should become clear...)

CE3_Wrong_Evidence_2: Umm... well, you see...{nl}H-Huh? Did I make a mistake?
CE3_Wrong_Evidence_4: Don't you have evidence showing something happened{nl}between 9:15 PM and the incident?

CE3_Right_Evidence_2_7: This brings up the question of why Mr. Sahwit had this in his possession...{p:400}{nl}And the answer is...
CE3_Right_Evidence_2_8: Right before the incident, {bg:speedlines}{playSound:bubble_sfx}{courtSprite:defense["ZoomIn"]}while the defendant was taking a shower,{nl}the one who stole her glasses was YOU, Mr. Sahwit!

CE3_Right_Evidence_2_22: Fine, fine! I'll admit that I dropped it{nl}at the murder scene!
CE3_Right_Evidence_2_24: ...{p:300}But let me tell you, I never went to the 4th floor{nl}at the time of the incident!
CE3_Right_Evidence_2_25: ...{p:300}B-But if you didn't go to the 4th floor,{nl}getting your hands on this would be...

CE3_Final_Right_Evidence_13: You returned to the 1st floor, and carried the victim,{nl}who died instantly from the severe blow to the back of the head, up to the 4th floor.
CE3_Final_Right_Evidence_14: You put the Steel Samurai mask on her{nl}and sat her on the railing.
CE3_Final_Right_Evidence_15: Then you sneaked into the waiting room{nl}and stole the showering defendant's glasses.
CE3_Final_Right_Evidence_16: You calculated that the defendant, with her poor eyesight,{nl}would push the victim just by touching her.
CE3_Final_Right_Evidence_17: After that, you waited on the 1st-floor stage.{nl}The rest is as everyone knows.
